opp.next_step: 19th May: Meeting 26th July 2026 onsite, alot of restructuring and they are currently trying to get a business case approved for a formal evaluation. Send follow-up email recap + Workday integration documentation (API/integration overview for IT). Schedule live demo (preferably onsite in Melbourne) focused on: Supplier Risk & Performance (CPS 230 / material service providers / 4th party) Contract renewals + compliance visibility (AI assist) Spend visibility by supplier (holistic view) Teams-based intake (reduce maverick buys) Share Horizon event invite (Apr 21–22) for Saud to consider/forward to Adam.
opp.ais_why: Netwealth Investments is a digital-first financial services organization with a strong focus on technology and operational efficiency. While there is no public evidence of a dedicated AI-led procurement transformation, the company has demonstrated continued investment in digital innovation and automation. Its procurement function is likely to benefit from AI-powered intake management, sourcing, contract lifecycle management (CLM), supplier risk management, and spend analytics to improve compliance, accelerate procurement cycles, and enhance decision-making. As AI adoption continues to grow across financial services, Netwealth is a strong candidate for evaluating Agentic AI-enabled Source-to-Pay (S2P) solutions.

metrics.narrative: The buyer has articulated the business problem in detail but has not quantified the financial or operational impact. Adam Baker (Procurement Manager) stated the pain explicitly: Netwealth is building procurement from scratch (started May 2024), managing ~498 suppliers manually via spreadsheets/emails/questionnaires, with limited visibility into compliance status, renewal timelines, and total spend per supplier (multiple engagements make it messy). The burning platform is APRA CPS 230 compliance ('consuming my life'), which requires material service provider controls, risk profiles, and monitoring. However, the CFO/CEO view is cost-skeptical: 'you can do it in a spreadsheet, and it's gonna take you an hour, or you can spend $100,000 a year on a platform, and it's gonna take you 45 minutes. I think we'll just keep using the spreadsheet' (source: Why_not_Zycus__c). No quantified ROI has been built (no cycle-time reduction, no cost savings number, no FTE impact, no audit-readiness dollar value). The business case is in progress but not yet approved, and without a quantified value case the EB approval gate remains high-risk. The next move is to co-build a quantified ROI framework (audit-readiness cost avoidance, risk-mitigation value, compliance staffing avoided, cycle-time saved) anchored to CPS 230 and their stated pain.

champion.narrative: Adam Baker (Procurement Manager, building the procurement function, pain owner, stated 'CPS 230 consuming my life') is the identified champion (source: X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, OpportunityContactRole Evaluator). However, Adam's engagement is passive and at risk. He dropped off a call on 2 April 2026 mid-session and has not re-engaged in 90+ days (source: Next_Step_History__c 19 May 2026). The last buyer touch was 21 May 2026, initiated by Saud Abdul Rahaman (Procurement Specialist, Influencer role), who stated priorities changed and they will reconnect once top management approves the business case (source: email task 21 May 2026). Adam has not responded to rep outreach since May. Saud is the active single thread but holds an Influencer role, not decision authority. Adam's cooling engagement signals either deprioritization, internal blocker (business case not approved, capacity consumed by Workday program), or champion strength weakening. Adam has access to the pain but NOT to the Economic Buyer (CFO/CEO unmapped), so his advocacy is insufficient to drive budget approval. Evidence of advocacy: Adam requested the demo and Workday integration documentation, articulated the pain in detail, and confirmed Zycus positioning ('one-stop view'). However, no introductions to EB/CFO/CEO, no internal champion moves visible, and no multi-threading progress. Champion strength is developing but at risk of stalling without EB engagement and business case approval.

competition.narrative: The primary competitor is Do Nothing / Internal Inertia, confirmed across multiple sources. Netwealth operated for 25 years without a procurement platform, and stakeholders initially questioned the need for a procurement function ('why do we need procurement?') (source: Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c). The CFO/CEO view is cost-skeptical: 'you can do it in a spreadsheet, and it's gonna take you an hour, or you can spend $100,000 a year on a platform, and it's gonna take you 45 minutes. I think we'll just keep using the spreadsheet' (source: Why_not_Zycus__c). The buyer may decide to wait until Workday stabilizes (Q3 2027) before adding another platform, or indefinitely delay if the business case is not approved (source: Why_not_Zycus__c). Competitors__c is set to 'Others' but Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c is null, and no named vendor competitor (Coupa, Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP) appears in any field, task, or Next_Step. How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c states they use 'mostly manual (spreadsheets, emails, questionnaires); finance paid invoices historically without procurement controls' and are implementing Workday (HR + Finance + basic PR-to-PO) but Workday is not a procurement platform competitor. The wedge against Do Nothing is to position Zycus as risk and compliance enablement (CPS 230 audit readiness, proactive monitoring, evidence trail) rather than time savings, co-build a quantified ROI framework that resonates with the CFO/CEO, and tie urgency to CPS 230 deadlines and audit cycles. If the business case is not approved or the CFO/CEO remains unconvinced, the deal will stall indefinitely.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is confirmed and articulated in detail by the buyer. Adam Baker (Procurement Manager) owns the pain and has stated it explicitly: Netwealth is building procurement from scratch (started May 2024), managing ~498 suppliers manually via spreadsheets, emails, and questionnaires, with limited visibility into who is compliant, renewal timelines, and total spend per supplier (multiple engagements make it messy). The burning platform is APRA CPS 230 compliance ('consuming my life'), which requires material service provider controls, risk profiles, fourth-party oversight, and monitoring. The company historically operated without a procurement function (25 years, finance paid invoices without procurement controls), and leadership initially questioned the need for procurement ('why do we need procurement?'), so Adam is building the function and the operating model from zero. The pain is operational (manual, time-consuming, error-prone) and regulatory (CPS 230 audit readiness, ASX scrutiny, PII/commercially sensitive data in systems). However, the CFO/CEO view is cost-skeptical ('spreadsheet vs $100k platform for small time savings'), so the pain owner (Adam) and the budget approver (CFO/CEO) are misaligned on urgency and value. The pain is real and documented but not yet translated into a quantified business case that resonates with the Economic Buyer.

paper_process.narrative: The paper process (contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec, SI/partner involvement) is unknown. No MSA/SOW discussion, no legal contact, no InfoSec review process, no procurement approval workflow, and no SI/partner role documented. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null (no incumbent contract forcing function). The buyer is building procurement from scratch and has no existing vendor relationship to replace, so there is no legacy contract to transition from. However, the buyer stated Workday integration is must-have (PR-to-PO, spend sync, finance GL) and requested API/integration documentation for IT review (source: Next_Step__c), which implies an IT sign-off gate exists but is not yet engaged. No IT contact is mapped. The paper process will likely involve: (1) IT integration approval, (2) InfoSec review (given APRA CPS 230 and PII/sensitive data concerns), (3) legal MSA/SOW redlining, (4) finance/procurement approval workflow. None of these are validated. The recommended move is to map the IT lead and InfoSec stakeholders with Adam/Saud, deliver the Workday integration documentation, and confirm the internal approval workflow and contracting timeline aligned to the November-December evaluation start.

Adam Baker (Procurement Manager) and Saud Abdul Rahaman (Procurement Specialist) are pain owners and operational stakeholders, but neither controls budget approval. Adam explicitly stated that business case approval is in progress and they are awaiting 'go ahead from the top management' (email 21 May 2026), and the CFO/CEO view on cost-justification is skeptical ('spreadsheet vs $100k platform'). The synthesis read across all sources confirms no C-level engagement: Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false, Who_will_approve_budget__c is null, no CFO/CTO/CEO in contact roles, and no C-level mentioned in tasks or Next_Step. decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially understood but not validated end-to-end. The buyer has stated that formal evaluation will start November-December 2026 AFTER Workday implementation stabilizes (15-month project completes Q3 2027), and they will not implement Zycus in parallel with Workday (source: Next_Step_History__c 19 May 2026). A business case approval gate exists and is in progress, awaiting 'top management' (CFO/CEO) approval before proceeding to formal evaluation (source: email 21 May 2026). The approval chain is not mapped: Who_will_approve_budget__c is null, Multiple_approvals__c is null, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c is null, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c is null. No multi-level approval process is documented. The contact-role sequence is single-threaded (Adam and Saud only), with no IT, legal, InfoSec, or C-level engagement to date. The realistic process appears to be: (1) business case approval from CFO/CEO, (2) demo and use-case validation, (3) RFP shaping November-December 2026, (4) Workday stabilization Q3 2027, (5) vendor selection and contracting. However, this is inferred, not confirmed by the buyer. What_is_the_decision_process__c is null. The recommended move is to map the full approval chain with Adam/Saud, confirm the November-December timeline and the Workday dependency, and secure agreement on the decision gates and stakeholders at each stage.

decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are partially articulated through the buyer's stated pain areas and demo focus but are not formalized or validated in a structured evaluation framework. The buyer requested a live on-site demo focused on four specific areas: (1) Supplier Risk & Performance (CPS 230 / material service providers / 4th party), (2) Contract renewals + compliance visibility (AI assist), (3) Spend visibility by supplier (holistic view), and (4) Teams-based intake (reduce maverick buys) (source: Next_Step__c). These four areas are the inferred evaluation criteria, driven by CPS 230 compliance, audit readiness, and operational control. Adam also stated they need a 'one-stop view' of supplier register, compliance status, risk profile, renewals, and spend visibility in one platform (source: Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c). However, no formal RFP, no BRD, no scoring rubric, and no validated decision criteria exist. Business_Requirements__c is null, Shoe_Fit_Criteria_Met__c is false, and no structured use-case validation or test-case sessions have occurred. The criteria are still emerging and not locked. The recommended move is to co-build a formal evaluation framework with Adam and Saud, mapping the four demo focus areas to quantified success metrics and CPS 230 audit requirements, and secure agreement that these are the decision gates.

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS Score 7.0, AIS Status Moderate. AIS_Why__c states Netwealth is digital-first, strong focus on technology and operational efficiency, continued investment in digital innovation and automation, and procurement function likely to benefit from AI-powered intake, sourcing, CLM, supplier risk, and spend analytics. Buyer explicitly requested 'AI assist' for contract renewals + compliance visibility (Next_Step__c), and articulated AI expectation as assistive acceleration with human review (aligned to Merlin positioning). However, no public evidence of AI-led procurement transformation, and CFO/CEO view is cost-skeptical, so AI appetite exists operationally (Adam/Saud) but is unproven commercially (EB level). AI Hungry signals (digital-first, innovation-focused, APRA compliance driver, AI use case articulated) are present but tempered by cost-justification pressure and business case approval gate. Tier: AI Curious (operationally interested, not yet strategically committed).

hard.ais_why: Netwealth Investments is a digital-first financial services organization with a strong focus on technology and operational efficiency. While there is no public evidence of a dedicated AI-led procurement transformation, the company has demonstrated continued investment in digital innovation and automation. Its procurement function is likely to benefit from AI-powered intake management, sourcing, contract lifecycle management (CLM), supplier risk management, and spend analytics to improve compliance, accelerate procurement cycles, and enhance decision-making. As AI adoption continues to grow across financial services, Netwealth is a strong candidate for evaluating Agentic AI-enabled Source-to-Pay (S2P) solutions.

value.name: SAP MM (Materials Management)
value.quote: Back in the days, we we still have that, actually. Back in the days, it was a one stop solution, the SAP MM, especially for procurement space that we have experienced. If not, like, SAP MM is really big and vast… for the requirements that we have, I think that is what we are looking for.
value.source: Avoma call 2 April 2026, Saud Abdul Rahaman
value.sentiment: neutral
value.how_we_win: Position Zycus as 'SAP MM scope (holistic source-to-pay, compliance, risk, AI) at mid-market deployment speed and price' — Saud's SAP reference sets the bar but also proves he knows enterprise platforms work, reducing Do Nothing credibility. Emphasize Workday integration (API, PR-to-PO, finance sync) as the modern equivalent to SAP's ERP integration, and show Teams-embedded intake + AI agents as faster, more user-friendly than SAP's complexity.

value.how_we_win: Differentiate on AI/Merlin (autonomous negotiation, AI-assisted compliance) vs SAP's legacy workflow-heavy MM module; emphasize CPS 230 fourth-party risk tracking depth and out-of-box Workday integration vs heavy SAP customization. Preempt SAP by locking Workday integration early (deliver API docs this week) and securing IT/InfoSec sign-off before SAP enters formal evaluation.

value.name: Do Nothing / Internal Inertia
value.quote: You can do it in a spreadsheet, and it's gonna take you an hour, or you can spend $100,000 a year on a platform, and it's gonna take you 45 minutes. I think we'll just keep using the spreadsheet (CFO/CEO view)
value.source: SF Why_not_Zycus__c
value.sentiment: negative
value.how_we_win: Position value as risk and compliance enablement (CPS 230 audit readiness, proactive monitoring, evidence trail, staffing avoided) rather than time savings. Co-build quantified ROI framework anchored to audit cost avoidance, regulatory risk mitigation, and control. Secure EB/CFO sponsorship by framing Zycus as enabling CPS 230 compliance at scale, not a productivity tool. Tie urgency to CPS 230 audit cycles and ASX scrutiny. Make business case approval the Phase 1 win.

value.name: Do Nothing / Internal Inertia
value.quote: Adam Baker stated CFO/CEO view: 'you can do it in a spreadsheet, and it's gonna take you an hour, or you can spend $100,000 a year on a platform, and it's gonna take you 45 minutes. I think we'll just keep using the spreadsheet.' Saud stated 'Netwealth operated 25 years without a procurement platform; initially questioned why we need procurement.'
value.source: Avoma discovery call 2 April 2026
value.sentiment: negative
value.how_we_win: Quantify CPS 230 audit cost avoidance (regulatory forcing function), contract leakage savings, and supplier consolidation value; position Merlin AI autonomous negotiation as capability multiplier that turns procurement into profit center, not cost center. Make this the centerpiece of the 26 July on-site demo with live ROI scenarios.

value.name: Do Nothing / Internal Inertia
value.quote: If I was our CFO or CEO, I'd be kinda like, well, you can do it in a spreadsheet and it's gonna take you an hour, or you can spend $100,000 a year on a platform and it's gonna take you 45 minutes. I think we'll just keep using the spreadsheet. So that's the current environment we're currently in.
value.source: Avoma discovery call 2 April 2026, Adam Baker
